profile.statement_to_voters:
I would like to introduce myself as a Conservative Candidate for the Holyrood Ward in the upcoming local elections.

I am proud to call Bury my home. 

I never thought I would come to rely on the help of others but when I arrived in the UK, I found myself in a situation where I desperately needed to be able to talk to someone.

When I arrived in the UK in 2008, I couldn't speak any English, but people were so kind and welcoming. I cried and cried tears of happiness because, in North Korea, I didn't feel welcome, but in the UK, people made me feel at home.

Now that the UK is my home, I am not just going to be the recipients of other people's kindness, I am also going to support others going through difficult situations.

I know last year had been one of the most difficult and challenging moments in our history but the light will always shine in the darkness.

Last year I supported local care homes and they said that they really applaud the refugees; coming to another country as an escapee and supporting another country's people with donations.

These words gave me courage and helped me support those many people who need us.

Winston Churchill said that "Courage is what it takes to stand up and speak, Courage is also what it takes to sit down and listen"

The reason I want to stand up as Local councillor is that I think residents need to have a lot of patience, be dedicated, passionate about becoming involved with the community and just generally interested in the local democracy and trying to make the community in which you live in much better. These are the qualities I possess.

If elected, I will be listening to our residents, our neighbours and I will protect our people.

I am your Bury Independent candidate for Moorside ward in Bury Council’s local elections due to take place on Thursday 2nd May 2024. I’d be really pleased if I could count on your vote this year, so that Bury Independents have a chance to show you how a local-only party can make a difference for you. There are no elections until 2026 after this one, so please consider lending me your vote on the 2nd May.

I am a resident of Moorside who would like to see change to better our community. I am a former primary school teacher and currently work within Children’s Services where I help serve the needs of families. In this role I see firsthand how lack of funding, a loss of focus on residents and the cost of living crisis can have a real impact on communities. I am passionate about building a better future for all of the residents in our area.

Labour failures in Bury and across the country are letting our communities down. A vote for the Green Party is a vote for hope and voice for real change.

My Priorities for Moorside:

Protect our environment:
* Care for our green spaces and campaign to protect our green belt
* Invest in our roads to repair the many potholes in the area
* Continue to tackle the problem of fly tipping

Invest in our schools and young people
* Increase local SEND provision so every child can thrive and achieve
* Provide more youth services and opportunities to engage in the local community
* Campaign for more mental health provisions for young people

Tackle the cost of living crisis
* Invest in community spaces from libraries, to food banks, to youth centres to allotments - improving access for all
* Prioritise affordable housing
* Champion the many amazing local businesses in our community

An independent voice for change
* Green councillors are not told how to vote
* I will be free to speak up for you and our community, holding the council to account
* I promise to work with a resident focus and listen to the needs of our community

I am a local man brought up on Dickie Bird Estate, owned and managed an Engineering Company for over 25 years in the Moorside Ward, recently retired.
I am the President of the Bury and District Bowling League, Treasurer of the Rotary Club of Bury.
I support a number of local charities.
I have seen the decline in the area under Labour Control and I want to make  a difference.
If elected I will serve the residents of Moorside to the best of my ability

profile.statement_to_voters:
I have lived and worked in Cambridgeshire for many years and I’m standing in the by-election to give you the chance to elect a councillor working for you. I firmly believe that to represent people you must take the time to knock on doors and listen. I have been out speaking to residents every day and will continue to meet as many residents as possible.

Local people say that Ramsey and Bury has been ignored by the County Council for far too long. The Council has been led by Conservatives or Lib Dems since it was established more than 50 years ago. Locally we’ve had Reform, Conservative and UKIP county councillors.

Now it’s your chance to change that. I will be an active and visible councillor, knocking on doors all year round and holding regular surgeries. I will work closely with our Labour MP, Sam Carling.

As a councillor, I will represent the whole community and my focus will be on fixing problems and delivering for Ramsey and Bury:

**Sort out our roads:** The Labour Government has doubled road repair funding. I will fight to ensure Ramsey gets its share and that fen roads get the special attention they need. I’ve been a county councillor before, I know how much should be spent here in Ramsey & Bury.

**The right infrastructure:** the Conservative mayor is threatening to cut over a third of our county’s bus network. The County Council has a direct role in being able to stop those plans.I will also campaign for better phone and WiFi connectivity here. With new housing being built locally, I’ll press for infrastructure first. It’s not fair on existing and new residents otherwise.

**Combat crime:** Clamp down on speeding and antisocial driving. More visible local police. More CCTV and better street lighting. Boost youth services so that young people have constructive things to do. I’ll organise public meetings for you to share your priorities with the police.

**Back our armed forces:** Labour is investing £1.5billion to improve housing for our armed forces. I will work with our MP to press for Ramsey service men and women to benefit.

**Tackle inequality:** I will campaign for more free breakfast clubs in our primary schools and stand against the Lib Dem council’s cuts to school holiday meals for the most vulnerable families. We need more places for children with special educational needs.

Time for a fresh start here in Ramsey and Bury. I believe in problem solving, not finger pointing. I am a hard-working and experienced councillor who is ready to fight for you from day one.

**Special message for ‘progressive voters’**

If you are a ‘tactical voter’ looking for the progressive option then it’s clear that Labour is your choice: Just over a year ago here in Ramsey and Bury, almost twice as many people voted Labour as backed either Lib Dem or Green.

Now a revitalised local party is mounting a strong campaign buoyed by recent opinion polls showing Labour nipping at the heels of Reform. It’s Reform we need to beat, they won the seat a year ago but already their councillor has resigned.

Let’s turn the page on past failures by electing me as your County Councillor.

**My name is Sharon Docherty, and I am the Liberal Democrat candidate for Ramsey and Bury.**

I live locally, and I am passionate about making this a place where everyone gets a fair deal — and I mean everyone.

I am a wheelchair user. I know first-hand the barriers people face when public services, transport and local infrastructure do not work for everyone — barriers that affect not just disabled people, but parents with young children and anyone without reliable transport. That lived experience shapes everything I stand for.

For too long, Ramsey and Bury have been taken for granted. Our Reform UK councillor quit after just one year, leaving us without representation and forcing this expensive by-election. You deserve better than that.

**If you elect me on 16th July 2026, I will aim to:**
- **Reduce the cost of living** — by making sure vulnerable residents get the support they are entitled to and fight for affordable local facilities
- **Provide access to banking** — by campaigning to bring a banking hub into Ramsey Library for older residents, disabled people and small traders
- **Push for better NHS provision** — by holding the health service to account and securing more GP and dental appointments closer to home
- **Improve the mobile signal** — by making sure Ramsey is at the front of the queue for improvement. It is on the county council's priority list for the national Shared Rural Network programme.

**What I bring:**
- **Over 20 years of senior Ministry of Defence experience, including policy and legislation work in Cyprus,** including planning and building legislation - negotiating with the Republic of Cyprus, the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus and the UK Foreign Office
- **Pastoral responsibility in the Falkland Islands for over 200 civil servants and 700 contractors,** covering everything from infrastructure to serious allegations of fraud and sexual assault
- **Doctoral research into workplace bullying, institutional betrayal and moral injury,** presented at the British Academy of Management 2026 Conference, University of London; Shaping the Future of Work Conference, King's College, London; and International Association on Workplace Bullying and Harassment Conference, Canberra, Australia
- **Panel membership contributing to the Timms Report** on PIP reform, representing the MS Society
- **FDA Union representative at the TUC Disability Conference,** championing Access to Work
- **Author of an FDA union report on Civil Service pay practices** causing detriment to disabled employees

**This is not just box-ticking for me.**

_I am passionate about fairness and providing a voice to those who do not them. And while I might be a new candidate, I believe my background demonstrates I know how to navigate complex systems, build coalitions and get things done. I am committed to bringing all of that experience and passion to work for Ramsey and Bury — while being open, accessible and in touch all year round, not just at election time._

The Liberal Democrats are winning here. In May 2026, we won more seats than any other party across Huntingdonshire. The momentum is with us. **A vote for Sharon Docherty on 16th July 2026 is a vote for real, positive change.**

Thank you for reading this. 
As you may know, I live in Ramsey. I love the area, the community and friendly people.
Thanks to you, I was elected to HDC to represent Ramsey and Bury. Much work is already underway and some of it is challenging and will take time, however change will come!
I am using my skills as a lawyer, to hold officers and other people to account to ensure fairness and justice for all. 
I'm standing again for ReformUK for you the people of Ramsey and Bury because you have been let down by successive governments, locally, at County and District levels, as well as Nationally. We need change and we need it now. 
If elected, I want to understand, scrutinise, challenge and review the current working practices of Cambridgeshire County Council at the same time as I continue my work on HDC in order to deliver improvements for local people through common sense. 
By holding both roles I can ensure the voice of Ramsey and Bury is best represented across both County and District Councils. Just as an example, pot holes and roads is a County matter. I also would like to build on the work of our former councillor James Sidlow at County level. 
With your vote, I will represent your voice at local  government level - County and District - to make sure the councils are accountable to you the electorate. 
The main 3 priorities coming from the doorsteps are:
1. Fix the roads 
2. Safer streets
3. Support small businesses
A vote for me as your Reform candidate is a vote for a chance at a better future for all in Ramsey and Bury, Cambridgeshire and Huntingdonshire.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Labour selects Benedict Allen to fight the Roman Bank and Peckover by-election

Cllr Benedict Allen, who lives locally says: it’s time we had a fresh start in Roman Bank and Peckover.  He already serves as a Parish Councillor and says he is determined to champion local voices, fight for the future of Wisbech and surrounding parishes, and work with local communities to restore pride.

Speaking at the campaign launch, Benedict said:

“Wisbech and Fenland are full of hardworking people who care deeply about their communities and want to see real change. But for too long, residents have felt overlooked. I know; I live here.

“I am standing because I believe we deserve better. Local people want representatives who will listen, stand up for them and deliver change. Together, we can build a stronger, fairer future for Wisbech and Fenland.

“This by-election won’t change who runs the County Council, it will still be the Liberal Democrats. It won’t even change who the official opposition is, that will still be the Conservatives.

“The by-election is a chance to vote for a candidate who is focused only on the local community. My pledges are:
I will be visible and available with all year round door-knocking and regular councillor surgeries
I will fight for our area to get its fair share of the £58m road repair budget and will campaign for higher quality so that pot holes are fixed once and for all.

“I already know how local councils work as I’m a parish councillor. If you put your trust in me as your county councillor, I will be effective from day one. I’ll be the voice of the community at the council, not the other way around as is too often the case.

A spokesperson for Fenland Labour said:

“This campaign is about hope, energy and rebuilding trust with communities that have too often been taken for granted. Benedict is passionate, approachable and deeply committed to standing up for the services communities rely on. We are excited to take that message directly to residents across Roman Bank and Peckover.”

The by-election, on Thursday 25 June, has been called after the previous Reform County Councillor was disqualified for breaking electoral law.

As a local and longstanding Wisbech resident, with a business in Leverington, I'm standing to represent Reform UK in Roman Bank and Peckover to speak up for the Division. Decades of previous Conservative administration have had a huge detrimental effect on roads and facilities within the division. I will champion local businesses and farmers, and put our local residents first. I am not a career politician and will push for common sense politics. Less waste, less talking and more action.

Too long, politics have felt distant to reality. Campaigns are run on National Politics level, with Party Policies the overriding message.
I don't come to this campaign with political experience.
I don't come with promises. 
I don't come with speeches and empty words.

I come with one aim.
To listen.
I will not claim I am aware of local issues that you may have. 
What affects me, may not affect you.
However, if you come to me with an issue, then I will listen.
Because what I want to know is this..
How can I help?

profile.statement_to_voters:
My name is Simran Patel, and I am running to be your Councillor. The recent national election confirmed to me, and I suspect many of you, that a new era is here - Labour is over, Reform is not welcome, and the future is Green. I am inspired by the momentum the party has gained, and am eager to build on this and effect change on the local level.

I live in Splott and thoroughly enjoy the vibrance of the local community, from Splo-down, to Oasis, to Railway Gardens. As a councillor, it would be a priority to strengthen networks and support these vital initiatives. I would also work to improve on infrastructure, specifically fixing potholes on our roads. As a keen environmentalist, I aim to make homes more energy efficient (thereby reducing bills for residents) and keep our streets clean and safe. Above all, I would ensure all residents' voices are heard. This role is a community service and I would be a reliable representative, holding regular surgeries and always being available and approachable.

My background is in Law and Sociology which gives me broad knowledge into the workings of local government. I have valuable experience in organising, public speaking, and have volunteered extensively for social justice causes such as the climate crisis, gender equality, migrant solidarity, and racial advocacy. I have always been intrigued by social politics; growing up as a working class, queer woman of colour provided me with a unique understanding of marginalisation, and how systems can help or hinder those they are meant to work for. I am passionate about collective action, and I will always stand strong against injustice.

In all honesty, I had not considered myself the right “type” of person to be a councillor. Very few in these positions look like me, a racialised queer woman at the start of her career. But as someone said to me recently, we need more “normal” people in politics. I know what it's like to worry about rising rent and bills, and I am no stranger to hard work given I am currently juggling multiple minimum wage hospitality jobs.

Therefore, I believe I would be a great councillor. Labour have had their time and now we need a change. A Green councillor would hold Labour to account, and wouldn’t be told what to do or say by a party that has to work in the interests of their billionaire donors. I bring with me a fresh hunger and a willingness to commit 100% of my time and energy to representing Splott, Tremorfa, and Pengam Green while continually reinforcing the values of the Green Party. I would be honoured for the opportunity to serve this community, and hope I have earned your vote to be your Councillor.

profile.statement_to_voters:
This will be the third time that Kelvin has contested the West Dorset constituency for the Green Party at a General Election, having stood in both 2017 and 2019. He is currently a councillor on Bridport Town Council, and has been a councillor on Dorset Council.

Prior to entering politics Kelvin worked as a firefighter for 17 years. On leaving the Fire Service he went to university as a ‘mature student’ and then re-trained to become a careers adviser. Whilst working as a careers adviser he continued his university studies on a part-time basis, finally gaining a PhD in philosophy. Philosophy has been his passion for many years, and he has a strong belief in the value of community philosophy and the teaching of critical thinking. He runs a local Philosophy in Pubs group.

If elected, what would be his priorities? “Well,” he says, “there is so much that needs changing it’s hard to know where to start. Obviously the government needs to start listening to the scientists and take climate and environmental issues seriously. It needs to drastically rethink its planning policies and start a programme that insures that all our homes are warm, dry and insulated. The UK has the worst quality housing stock in Europe. And it needs to take all public utilities back into public ownership – it’s scandalous that some people are making a fortune out of the energy market whilst others can barely afford to keep warm.”

Which is pretty much what you would expect from a Green Party candidate. But is there anything else he would like to change? “Yes,’ he replies, “we need to start doing politics differently. This should include the obvious move to proportional representation, but also the restructuring of Parliament to make it less confrontational. We need to start having open and honest discussions, and we need to open-up these discussions to as many people as possible. I would like to see a move towards greater public participation in decision making.”

I’m standing as the Green Party candidate for North Acton in the by election because I believe ordinary people – not career politicians – deserve a stronger voice.

After nearly 19 years in Ealing, I’ve seen poor council decisions: threats to Warren Farm, overdevelopment without affordable housing, neglected green spaces, and failure to support residents affected by projects like HS2.

This must change. Ealing should work for everyone, and more Green councillors will mean real collaboration with residents.

I’ve previously stood in local and London Assembly elections and bring experience in community organising, climate activism, citizens’ assemblies, and social justice work. I cycle everywhere, support a local animal rescue, and work in nature conservation. In my professional life I have managed a variety of teams, worked in media and press teams, community-based projects and campaigning, mostly under high pressure and chaos – I’d say I am ready to face the duties of a councillor with confidence.

I want to bring transparency and accountability to Ealing, as well as listening to residents’ needs and concerns and building a plan together to improve lives, neighbourhoods and communities.

candidates.ballot_description: Ealing Community Independents - Putting Acton First
candidates.name: Craig Smith

profile.statement_to_voters:
Craig Smith is leader of Ealing Community Independents – a new political party, here to contest the local elections on May 7 and bring fresh ideas to the borough.

For too long, Ealing Council has been run by a single party that consistently fails residents: they break promises, they don't listen, they fail to consult properly, and they cut our public services while increasing council tax.

>>>

Craig has lived in Ealing for more than 25 years – helping bring up two children through local authority schools. A proud community campaigner and trade unionist, he is committed to equality, justice, peace and human rights.

He believes key local decisions should be participatory – shaped with, not done to, residents and workers.

Craig is a founder of the Save Ealing Children’s Centres campaign, a former school governor, and serves as an officer of Ealing Trades Union Council. He is a former business journalist and now strategic communications director, with experience working collaboratively at a senior level within large, matrixed organisations.

>>>

Ealing Community Independents is a people-led local party committed to putting residents’ voices and choices first, in every neighbourhood from Acton to Southall.

We exist to challenge a council culture that answers upward to Westminster parties instead of outward to local people. We will replace it with accountable, independent, community-rooted representatives.

Ealing Community Independents believe the current system is broken and that existing political parties are not doing enough to support everyone in our society.

We will offer the people of Ealing an alternative at the next council elections in May 2026. We will be your voice, your party, representing your choices. We will commit to bring public services, jobs and power back to those who have been neglected.

Ealing Community Independents stand for investment in public services – not cuts, closures or privatisation. We stand for equality and social justice. We will champion social housing and investment for our youth.

I am standing because I believe North Acton deserves better.

I am a lifelong Ealing Borough resident. I work for a Liberal Democrat Member of Parliament as a Parliamentary Researcher, focusing on issues such as local government finance, public services and cost-of-living pressures. Previously, I worked on a £48 million UK Foreign Office programme supporting greener, more resilient cities.

As a fluent Somali speaker, I am able to engage with and represent North Acton’s diverse communities. Through my work in Parliament, I have experience dealing with policy casework, helping residents get answers and solutions.

Local residents feel that they are paying more and getting less in return. Ten of the borough’s children’s centres are being closed, including Maples Children’s Centre. Residents are dealing with crime and anti-social behaviour, dirty streets, and cuts to essential public services despite Council Tax rising year after year.

I believe things do not have to be this way. I want a council that listens to residents, treats people with respect and puts local communities first. I will continue campaigning to save our children’s centres, work with the police and local residents to tackle crime and anti-social behaviour, and hold the council to account for the quality of local services.

I am also passionate about ensuring that young people in North Acton have the opportunities they need to succeed. I want to see more investment in opportunities that help young people of all backgrounds develop skills, find employment and fulfil their potential.

As Ealing Council’s Official Opposition, the Liberal Democrats are the only party able to provide the scrutiny needed to hold those in power to account and ensure residents’ voices are heard. I will be a strong voice for North Acton, standing up for local people and working every day to make our area a better place to live in.

The Ealing Conservatives are delighted to announce that Sally Gorman has been selected as the Conservative candidate for the North Acton by-election. This by-election  has been triggered by the newly-elected Green Party councillor quitting days after the local elections. This is costing you, the taxpayers tens of thousands of pounds.

Sally, who has lived in the area for thirty years, is standing to put North Acton residents’ priorities first: a safer, cleaner and more prosperous Acton.

On 7th May, voters kicked out Labour from North Greenford, who had held the seats for more than 30 years and elected Conservative Councillors to replace them.

The Conservatives can beat Labour locally.

The choice isn’t just between Keir Starmer’s failing Labour Council and the dangerous Greens who want higher taxes, legalisation of all drugs and who are weak on crime.

This by-election is about who will be a vocal and effective voice for Acton on the Council.  Sally will stand up and be that voice. It is time for change in Acton.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Gateshead’s newly elected Reform council has found to its own cost that 11 days is a long time in politics. That’s how long it took for one of their councillors in the High Fell ward to resign.

A lot of people are rolling their eyes at the lame excuse for the resignation. In a letter sent out to households – not even written by the councillor throwing in the towel – it cites that her business interests have now grown across the south of England. So clearly she doesn’t have time for us in High Fell!

We have been out on campaign stalls and knocking on doors to canvass support for our Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition (TUSC) candidate in the by-election.

The only hostile response we have had was from a woman who thought we were Labour. Once she realised we weren’t, she was interested in our policies, including opposing all cuts in council services.

Just before we knocked one young woman’s door, a Reform leaflet had been pushed through her door. She had immediately pushed it back outside. We found it on the ground!

The young woman was very interested in what we put forward. We’re hoping she gets involved in our campaign.

We’ve had quite a few people come up to say they’ve voted for us for years, including a retired member of Unite. He supports Sharon Graham, the union’s general secretary. But wished she’d stop funding Labour, rather than just threatening to.

Our campaign stalls are attracting young people. One 16-year-old told us everyone else in his house was Reform, but he definitely was looking to socialist ideas.

Even people who said they had voted Reform, and intend to do so again at the 9 July by-election, were prepared to listen to us. Many of these people could definitely be won over by a mass workers’ party, fighting for socialist policies.

For the past 20 years, I've had the privilege of calling Chesterton home and now I’d like the opportunity to serve our community as your local District Councillor.

That’s why I’m delighted to be standing in the upcoming district council by-election backed by the local Liberal Democrats.

I’m originally from America, and after moving to Chesterton with my husband Lee, we raised our children here. They attended Chesterton Primary School and Deer Park School, and many of you may know them from their time working at Tesco on Chesterton Lane!

I’ve spent my career in both the public and private sectors, including serving as an officer for Cotswold District Council, running my own business and working in waste management which is helpful for making sure the bins are collected!

I understand our community because I’ve experienced many of the issues myself that people worry about.

As former Chair of Governors at Chesterton Primary School and a current Town Councillor, I’ve worked hard to serve our community. Now, I want to bring that experience to the District Council to make Chesterton an even better place to live.

I'm standing with Reform UK in Cirencester Park and St Michael's to ensure that the local people of our town have a voice arguing for common-sense and for the basic essentials of our county government to be done as well and as efficiently as possible.

I'm a lifelong resident and engineer working for a small local business and as a father of two in Cirencester I'm glad that I'm raising them in what is a lovely and welcoming town.

Yet in a town like ours with its outward prosperity and rich heritage and in our country which can sometimes be so generous with its welfare spending it is easy to forget that for the working families of this town who receive no help or handouts life has become ever harder.

In this difficult time for our country my priority will always be to ensure that the council tax payers of Cirencester get a service worthy of the amount they are asked to pay for it and that their money is spent wisely, focusing on the duties that benefit all of us and not on showy, feel-good projects.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Biography:

Born, bred, brought up and educated in the UK. I have worked and visited in all but one continent. I was state-educated.

I have lived in this constituency since 1987 – I am not a candidate parachuted in! I have always been concerned about this area and its people.

I have been a town councillor, the mayor, and a trustee of several local charities. I have volunteered and helped on several occasions for various community projects.

I was an ardent independent, but Reform does not dictate their policies to representatives if the policy does not benefit their constituency.

I believe in:

Drain the Swamp, not the People.

Support the country you live in – or – live in the country you support.

Don’t judge people by what group they’re in – but by their individual actions and words.

My other concerns include how the governments have let us down – the people.

Governments for decades have let us down.

The Tories Broke Britain - Labour will bankrupt Britain - Only Reform UK can Save Britain.

The one thing that I feel the various governments have really let us down is trust. Our armed services lay down their lives for ‘King and Country’.

Some of them then get prosecuted for their actions whilst in uniform. For example, Kier Starmer prosecuted Soldiers for a false claim of brutality in Iraq.

The government repays those brave people who have poorly maintained married quarters. And a lack of support for our retired veterans.

Last year, the number of homeless veterans rose by 14%; nearly ½ of them slept on the streets. (the rest in shelters).

I think it’s time for the government to look after our troops.

Whilst serving their welfare and for their lawful actions whilst serving and not protecting those who acted against our troops.

I lived in Woodmarket for over 30 years since moving here in 1984. I support Lutterworth Rugby and Cricket teams and I was Treasurer of the Cricket Club for 13 years. As Chair of the Town Hall Charity I obtained grants for the new lift and repainting the building - a task that needs doing regularly due to the pollution caused by the excessive traffic in the centre of Lutterworth.
 
I am very concerned about the plans for Lutterworth East which means that Lutterworth will not get an effective North South bypass that is urgently needed to stop traffic blighting the town. I recently asked a Freedom of Information request to Leicestershire County Council – apparently, they have already spent £39.4m on the project and may only go ahead with more warehousing on the fields on the other side of the M1.  The proposed Misterton Quarry will exacerbate traffic issues without a by-pass.
 
Local representatives shouldn’t be dependent on party politics but on having someone who will effectively represent you. Having said that, many local elections recently have been decided on national issues with extremists of both sides making gains. I believe that we should work with others rather than against them. I wish we were back in the EU but if not should be in the single market and want fairness to all, especially minority groups, and electing me is a small way of supporting this approach.

My name is Adam, the Green Party candidate for Lutterworth East Ward.  I have lived in South Leicestershire for the past 13 years.  I have a background working in graphic design and ICT.  Having worked in our NHS, I have a passion to protect it.

As a husband and father, I am deeply concerned with the state of the world.  Like many of us, I am unhappy with the growing inequality and increasing division in our society.  For many families, it feels like things just keep getting worse.

I’m proud to stand on behalf of the Green Party because I want to bring real hope to our community in Lutterworth.  We offer a positive alternative to the traditional parties, who have let us all down.

I will work hard for Lutterworth and call for the improved infrastructure the town needs.  I will help cut down on traffic through the promotion of active travel and regular, reliable bus services. I want to support the Police in reducing anti-social behaviour.  I will take steps to protect our environment, clean up our waterways, and fight for much needed investment in our NHS through proper developer contributions.

Whatever the result on July 2nd, I’ll always be available to listen to your concerns and lend my support towards a better Lutterworth.

Martin Cullen: Your Labour Party Candidate for County Council.

Hello, my name is Martin Cullen, and I am honoured to stand as the Labour Party candidate in the upcoming County Council elections.

I have called Leicestershire home since 2002, when I moved here to attend the University of Leicester. For the past five years, I have lived in our wonderful community with my wife, Sarah, and our six-year-old twin daughters, who attend Gilmorton Primary School. Since September 2024, I have proudly served as a parent governor at their school.

Professionally, Sarah and I run a successful events company. Beyond our business, I am deeply involved in our community through various voluntary roles. I serve on the governing board at the University of Leicester and as a Parish Councillor in Gilmorton, where I am dedicated to supporting our residents and enhancing our village.

A lifelong Labour supporter, I have been an active party member since 2019. Recently, I had the privilege of supporting our South Leicestershire candidate, Robert Parkinson, in the 2024 General Election.

Living in and around Lutterworth, I am passionate about our community and spend a lot of time in the town and surrounding villages. I am excited to campaign for the position of your County Councillor, committed to engaging with residents meaningfully and regularly to be a strong voice for Lutterworth on the County Council.

Through my campaigning, I have identified key issues that matter to the people of Lutterworth, including the impact of the proposed Lutterworth East developments, traffic concerns, and residents' parking near the town centre.

If elected, my commitment is clear: I will be accessible year-round, ensuring it is easy to contact me through various methods that suit you. I will also hold monthly pop-up sessions, providing opportunities for you to speak with me in person.

In recent years, our Labour District councillors have delivered on important issues such as green spaces and housing. I look forward to meeting as many of you as possible in the coming months and earning your support.

My goal is to be your County Councillor, a strong voice for Lutterworth, dedicated to improving our community and the lives of its residents.

Thank you, Martin

profile.statement_to_voters:
Born and raised in Thanet, I attended Hereson School before settling in Broadstairs with my partner and our two bulldogs, Harold and Bertie. Living here, I see firsthand the challenges our community faces – especially when it comes to traffic, road conditions, and community safety.

My career began in retail, working at Waitrose in Ramsgate before joining the John Lewis commercial sales team in London. After 11 years in the industry, I have recently moved into marketing and events, working for a business in Whitstable. This experience has given me a strong background in problem-solving, customer service, and getting things done – skills I will bring to the County Council.

I believe residents deserve better value for the taxes they pay. That means pushing for more funding from Kent County Council and ensuring our community gets its fair share. Our streets must be safer, and I will work with local officers and Kent’s Police and Crime Commissioner to reduce the petty crime that far too often gets overlooked and leads to worse anti-social behaviour down the line.

As a St. Peter’s resident, I know just how bad the congestion is – especially on Westwood Road during school drop-off times. It’s time for planners to see the problem firsthand and take action. I’m also determined to improve our roads. If elected, I will conduct an annual pothole survey and ensure repairs are made.

Above all, I believe councillors should represent your views, not just repeat the council’s line. I will always stand up for Broadstairs and fight for our community’s interests.

Hello, my name is Rob Yates, I live in Margate and work in Ramsgate for an offshore wind farm. I am passionate about solving real local issues, like the sewage released on our beaches, landlords who are failing our neighbours, rubbish on our streets, and the lack of jobs locally. As a business-friendly councillor, I hope to continue to serve the residents and businesses of Margate Central in order to protect what makes Margate so special and attract further sustainable investment into the town.  I am a keen environmentalist and proposed the 2030 climate emergency motion at Thanet District Council. However, more can be done. In Australia for example, there are incentives for solar panel installation on low-income renters’ homes, the residents who most need the reduction in their bills, and I would like to investigate this further if elected.

I am proud to serve the residents of Margate Central and it would be my honour to continue to do so, ensuring Margate residents get the attention I know they deserve.

Young people are at the heart of my campaign because they are the key to building a healthier, happier, thriving society. To ease the strain on health and social care, we must focus on preventative measures, starting with substantial investment in maternity, children’s and youth services. Every child deserves the best start in life, empowering them to grow into active, responsible citizens who contribute positively to their communities.

As a town and district councillor, I am already dedicated to making Broadstairs a cleaner, safer, kinder place to live, work and visit. I’ve built strong relationships with local schools, established the Broadstairs Youth Forum to amplify young voices, and led initiatives that prioritise community cohesion and inclusion, such as working towards a dementia-friendly town. With over a decade of experience working in schools, I’ve been a passionate advocate for education, championing better pay for teachers and support staff.

As your Kent county councillor, I will build on this work. My key pledges include expanding youth and children’s services, improving SEND provision, making roads safer, enhancing public transport, reducing anti-social behaviour and violence against women and girls (VAWG), and promoting environmental sustainability. I will also push for better funding for health and social care, with a focus on mental health support.

I believe investing in young people and education is the wisest choice we can make. Your vote on May 1 will enable me to make a meaningful difference in our community and start to build a society where everyone thrives.

Dr Mo Shafaei is proud to stand as a Liberal Democrat candidate for Cliftonville,
Margate in the next local election. With a Master’s and PhD in Environmental
Sciences and a career as an Analytical Chemist, he brings a practical, evidence-
based approach to tackling the challenges our community faces. Having lived in
Margate for almost a decade, he understands the issues affecting the residents and
am committed to making a real difference.
 
If elected as your councillor, he will focus on three key issues:
 
1. Affordable and Quality Housing – Many residents face high rents, overcrowding,
and poor housing conditions. He will push for fairer rental policies, more social
housing, and action against rogue landlords to ensure everyone has access to a safe
and decent home.
 
2. Better Local Services and Healthcare – GP shortages, long waiting times, and
underfunded social care are serious concerns. He will fight for better healthcare
access, stronger social services, and more support for elderly and vulnerable
residents to improve well-being in our community.
 
3. A Cleaner, Greener Cliftonville – Our streets, parks, and coastline deserve better
care. He will work to tackle littering, improve recycling services, and promote
sustainable transport to make Cliftonville a cleaner, healthier place to live.
 
As a Liberal Democrat, he believes in fairness, transparency, and accountability. He
will regularly engage with residents to ensure your voices shape the decisions that
affect Cliftonville. In his word, “together, we can build a stronger, fairer, and greener
community. I ask for your support in making this vision a reality.”

We are delighted to announce that Alex Davies has been selected as the Liberal Democrat candidate for St Leonards in the upcoming By Election!  The election was caused by a candidate elected as a Green declining to take up her position

Alex was born and raised in Streatham and became a dedicated local campaigner in the area. He served on Lambeth Council as a councillor before working for the Lib Dems as Vince Cable's chief of staff.

Alex has campaigned on a wide variety of local issues, like:

- Saving the Thameslink service to Streatham and Tulse Hill,
    - Stopping the closure of Streatham Library,
    - Renovating Streatham High Road with paving stones and trees.

This by election is going to be close. Alex and his team have been out knocking on hundreds of doors and meeting residents since the by election was called and it is very clear people are annoyed that they have been let down by the Greens and forced into having another election so soon after May 7.

Not only could this by election cost tax payers around £25,000*, it will also force many residents into once again having to arrange childcare because their school needs to operate as a polling station. This isn't fair and something needs to be done so it doesn't happen again.

I’ve lived in Lambeth for several years as a local private renter, where I and a rotating cast of flatmates have put down roots as best we can. I’m a keen gardener, although I”m not very good yet.

I’ve joined and helped lead campaigns for a fairer, kinder society that puts people and planet first. As a union rep, I joined efforts to organise under-unionised workplaces with IWGB – especially for migrant workers, who are particularly vulnerable to exploitation from their employers. Especially now, with a rising far right and all our rights under attack, we need to organise together for safe workplaces and to protect each other.

As a climate activist with Green New Deal Rising, I’ve been part of demands for a wealth tax, proper investment in a green transition, and better future for young people from sit-ins outside the Treasury and protests outside Reform UK’s headquarters to donning a monopoly man head to expose the firms lobbying for Britain’s super rich (the head was difficult to see out of).

I’ve worked on anti-inequality campaigns for years, helping create action plans for local government that can make our neighbourhoods more equal, APPG reports on the cruel impacts of proposed benefit cuts, and research papers outlining the enormous costs of the UK’s high inequality.

I’m truly passionate about reducing inequality of wealth and power, and especially in Lambeth – one of the most unequal areas in the UK – I want to see Lambeth actual building social homes and local wealth, instead of begging for scraps from private developers and the sovereign wealth fund of Abu Dhabi; real power over our own lives with projects like participatory budgeting; and residents given a real voice, whether that’s over the long neglect of Streatham High Road or action on atrocities like Gaza.

I’m campaigning on issues like reopening the Streatham police base, tackling the betting and vape shops on the High Road or the empty ones around Greyhound Lane, and making our public spaces inviting and green.

I’m standing to be your next Conservative Party Councillor for
Streatham St Leonard’s.

I believe in value for money, but Labour in Lambeth has failed
residents: higher council tax with little improvement in services,
poor police communication, and a neighbourhood left behind on
investment, cleanliness, and basic standards, with Serco not de-
livering for St Leonard’s.

We are paying more and getting less: fortnightly bin collections,
rising fly-tipping, anti-social behaviour on Streatham Green and
the High Road, and no meaningful improvement.

I’ve lived in Streatham for over a decade, work locally, serve in
the Reserve Maritime Force, and referee football across London.
I care deeply about this area and am standing because I want to
act, not stand by.

Kind regards,
Chris Cousins

As your council and your elected representatives, we will always have to make difficult decisions, some of which may not be popular but I am proud to be part of the leading group that has always cast their votes, been prepared to stand up and be counted; and always taken the decisions that are right for the people who live, work and play in the Blaby area.

In Blaby district, we are privileged to be seen as a golden economy for investment and employment and these investments and developments will bring amazing opportunities for generations to come; not just with local jobs and skills but with access to brand new state-of-the-art leisure, education and health services; investment and improvements to motorway junctions and a wide range of mixed and affordable housing for local people”.

Starting his career as an Apprentice at GEC in New Parks; Les quickly moved on to enjoy a career in a leading FTSE100 Plc and most latterly a major USA Group bringing him into contact with amazing people from all walks of life. Now; as a Co-Director of a local business consultancy, Regional Chairman for the Federation of Small Businesses, Clerk to Cosby Parish Council and volunteer Mentor for students from year 11 upwards through to Masters level; Les says: -

“I am privileged to be able to help others succeed by pursuing my passion for business, innovation, skills and people. I get to work with a wide variety of businesses; universities and experts and represent thousands of small businesses across the East Midlands; ensuring that their voices are heard with senior stakeholders; politicians and Government agencies”.

As a mature business person and ex-job hunter, Les is himself profoundly aware of the challenges faced by the over 50’s who are finding re-employment challenging and the enormous impacts that can have on family life. Not only does Les champion this cause at every opportunity but with a small team supporting him, Les launched a new recruitment and placement job board specialising in the over 50’s who possess immense business and economic expertise and have the experience to identify new opportunities, attract investment; build businesses and create jobs. Already partnered with the DWP, Les is in discussions with Higher Education and global research groups about the challenges and opportunities for the over 50’s and collectively, we look forward to making a real difference and lobbying Government."

I’ve lived locally all my life, having been born in Cosby and a Whetstone resident since 1997. I previously stood as the Green Party candidate here in 2021, and I’m proud to be standing again to offer a strong, independent voice for our communities.

Our area deserves better than traffic congestion, unreliable public transport, and streets that don’t feel safe for walking and cycling. I’ve spent years campaigning for realistic alternatives, including better buses, safer active travel routes, and planning that puts people first. The best way to reduce congestion is to give people real, affordable choices.

I also believe local government should be transparent and accountable. Residents should understand what decisions are being made and have a genuine say in shaping them.

Alongside my campaigning, I bring professional experience as a solution architect, delivering complex transformation projects. I will use those skills to help the council make better, more informed decisions for the future.

If elected, I will work hard for you, your family, and our community.

I am standing in this by-election because I believe our community deserves a councillor who will listen to residents, stand up for local priorities and work hard to deliver practical solutions.

I have lived and worked professionally in the local area for many years and have strong personal ties to Narborough, Littlethorpe and Whetstone. My family and I live locally and I support ageing relatives in the area, which means the future of our community matters deeply to me.

County councillors make important decisions about roads, flood management, education, social care and local services - decisions that affect every household in our villages.

✔ Act – work constructively to deliver practical solutions

profile.statement_to_voters:
I was born in Luton and raised in Wigmore, and I am proud to still call this community my home. Standing as your candidate is deeply personal to me because I care about the future of the place that shaped my life.

In recent years, more and more people have recognised that the Green Party offers something different. We are not just about protecting the environment, we are about doing politics differently, with practical, people-focused policies that can genuinely transform our country for the better.

Wigmore is facing serious challenges, and I believe it is time for a strong local voice that will stand up and act.

Our ward is under real threat from large-scale, unrestricted developments. The expansion of the airport and associated plans risk fundamentally changing the character of our area. Proposed changes, such as turning Wigmore Lane into a four-lane road past Wigmore Primary School, raise serious concerns for safety, congestion, and quality of life.

At the same time, new housing developments are being planned without the necessary infrastructure. With over 2,100 homes potentially relying on already congested roads through Wigmore, the pressure on our community will only increase.

We are also facing an ongoing air pollution crisis. Traffic congestion must be reduced and managed responsibly, not pushed deeper into residential areas. If we do not act now, Wigmore risks becoming unrecognisable from the place we know and love.

My Priorities and Commitments

If elected, I will work tirelessly for a fairer, greener, and healthier Wigmore. My key pledges are:

1. Protect Wigmore from overdevelopment: I will challenge inappropriate large-scale developments and push for proper planning that puts residents first.

2. Oppose unsafe road expansion: I will stand against plans that put children and families at risk, including the proposed widening of Wigmore Lane near schools.

3. Tackle traffic and congestion: I will campaign for smarter transport solutions that reduce congestion and keep heavy traffic away from residential streets.

4. Fight air pollution: I will push for urgent action to improve air quality and protect the health of our community.

5. Stand up for local residents: I will ensure that any new housing developments come with proper infrastructure and do not overwhelm existing communities.

6. Support health and wellbeing: I will advocate for better investment in the NHS, social care, and local health services.

7. Address inequality and poverty: I will work to reduce the inequalities that affect too many lives in our area.

8. Invest in young people and skills: I will support training and opportunities in green industries, giving our young people hope and a future.

I will work constructively with others on Luton Council to deliver real change. Our community deserves better, and I am committed to being a strong, honest voice for Wigmore.

I hope to make history as the first Green Party councillor in Luton, building on the growing support for a new kind of politics that puts people, community, and the future first.

From Wigmore, for Wigmore.

Hi, my name is James Fletcher - Reform UK Spokesperson for Luton North.

I’m not a politician, just a working-class UK citizen who knows our country is being destroyed from the inside, We have given Labour & Conservatives enough time over the years with silent acceptance of how our country was ran and it’s time for this to change.  The current leadership of both are destined failures, who truly don’t deserve our vote.

This Tory government is actively harming our country, destroying our history, heritage and consider themselves above scrutiny and accountability.

This Labour party have proved themselves a poor opposition, weak and ineffectual whilst at times acting in unison with government policies that have been detrimental to the people to our United Kingdom.

We need drastic change for the better in the 2024 general election, Our United Kingdom needs to elect as many people from outside the 2 main parties as possible, the circle does not have an automatic right to govern our country and change is now possible.

For a number of years now, I have watched Luton Labour play diversity and division politics, and with a clear lack of opposition to push back on their policies, it leaves many Luton North Residents in desperate need of representation because they won’t & don’t support Labour and can no longer offer support to the Conservative party.

With that, I am delighted to be selected as the Reform UK PPC for Luton North, I believe Reform UK are the only common-sense option in the room and come General Election time it will be clear that Reform UK are the only party listening to the people.

I have been a Luton North Resident for 8years, I live here with my family who work and go to school in Luton.  Luton North is my home. 

Luton North deserve a candidate that understands the issues they are facing.

Luton North needs an MP who will challenge this lazy Labour run local council, an MP prepared to challenge Labour’s vested interests, an MP who will challenge, not cover-up, Labour wrongdoing, an MP that will be a voice and representative for all of Luton.

We will need stronger representation in Parliament if we want a Safer Luton, a Fairer Luton and a Luton we want to be proud of.

I am fully committed to ditching Net Zero and will campaign to give people a choice on this via a referendum.

I am fully committed to ditching the woke agenda engulfing us all.

I am fully committed to highlighting corruption and negligence in public office.

A vote for me is a vote for secure, safe and clean streets.

A vote for me is a vote for an honesty, transparency and common sense.

A vote for me is a vote against closed shop politics.

A vote for me is a vote against Net Zero, DEI and Woke Politics.

A vote for me is a vote to Save our Country and Take it Back.

I would never presume to know every issue of the area, but I would give 100% time and commitment to assist with the most compelling and identified electorate issues that arise, giving a voice to every single person who needs it, or wants it.

I will support local community organisations to be strong and successful. creating a fairer Luton North where every community thrives.

I will campaign to make extra funding available to increase activity/educational programs at community/drop-in centres for young adults whilst demanding greater support and services from government at a pivotal time in their lives.

I will campaign to make extra funding available for SEN & SEND schools in Luton North giving children with learning difficulties much greater help in there early years.

I will campaign for better quality services, a better transport network and increased investment for our local parks and green spaces.

I will acknowledge the homeless/begging problem on the streets of Luton North and put in place plans to tackle this issue.

I will solve the pothole problem that plague’s roads in Luton North. 

I will be visible and accessible to all residents of Luton North and put the community needs at the forefront of any decisions made that will affect them.

Dear Resident,

I’m Charlie Anderton, I’m your Green Party candidate for the by-election in Alfrick, Leigh and Rushwick ward taking place on 21 May, and I’d like to introduce myself.

I live locally and work for an environmental data charity helping to hold large companies to account on their environmental performance.

Before I moved to the area, I was a Parish councillor in my home village.  I valued being part of a thriving local community and  I became a Parish councillor because I wanted to give something back.  I contributed my enthusiasm and energy, working with other councillors to make the village a better place to live for everyone.

Since moving here, I have been working with the Wildlife Trusts on their youth Environmental Leadership Program for the West Midlands.  The programme supports young people from under-represented and underserved communities across the West Midlands.  It aims to give young people the skills to enable them to consider careers working with wildlife or the environment.

Outside of my work, I also volunteer for UK Youth for Nature.  This is a youth-led campaigning organisation calling for urgent action on the nature crisis.  Through this organisation I have been advocating for bolder action to remove water pollutants, forever chemicals and pesticides in public spaces.  As part of these campaigns, I have hosted information sessions for MPs and have a better understanding of how our political system works and how to effect change. I work a lot with youth organisations and see that too often young people can’t access the opportunities they need and deserve.

**I will speak up for Alfrick, Leigh and Rushwick residents**

I have decided to stand as a District Councillor so I can be a strong voice for everyone in our area.  My experience as a Parish Councillor has given me a good understanding of Planning and how meaningful  consultation with residents works. Key to this is identifying local needs and issues.  I am a strong believer that good public transport connections should be a key consideration in housing development as well as ensuring developers deliver what they have promised for the community. I will fight to ensure that any development provides a net benefit to people and planet and make sure your voice is heard.

**I can win here**

In 2025, the last Worcestershire County Elections, the Green Party won the popular vote in Malvern Hills District. Lots of people are telling us they will be voting Green this time as they don’t want Reform to win.   
The Reform-led Worcestershire County Council has increased our council tax by almost 9%, adding to the cost of living pressures for residents in Alfrick, Leigh and Rushwick.

**I am independently minded and will speak up for you.**

I have the experience, energy and enthusiasm to be your voice.  My experience as a Parish Councillor has given me the skills to be both independently minded and able to cooperate in a wider group  when the common goal is to benefit residents.  Councillors from other parties vote as they are told by their political party. This will 
never happen to me.   
I do not want to take part in petty politics. I am here to represent you.  
With your support I pledge to do my best for you. 
My promise to you: 
If elected, I will: 
• Listen to you, act and keep you informed 
• Attend council meetings to ensure you are represented and your voices are heard
• Make sure our council services are accessible and affordable to all residents
• Be the strong voice for everyone in our area
• Work to improve facilities with proper consideration of public transport and other 
sustainable travel opportunities
 • Protect our green spaces

Yours faithfully 
Charlie Anderton

Nick grew up in the Tyne Valley, and attended local schools including Queen Elizabeth High School in Hexham. 

Nick has over 20 years of experience working in Higher Education as a History lecturer. He currently works with distance learners at the Open University and international students at St Andrews University. He teaches courses from Foundation to Postgraduate level. 

Nick recently stood down after 24 years as a Newcastle City Councillor to fight the Hexham election. He brings with him a wealth of experience from his time in Council politics. Under Nick’s watch, as Cabinet member for Children’s Services, Newcastle achieved Beacon Status for Tackling and Preventing Child Poverty. He was more recently a Liberal Democrat Member of the Local Government Association's Children & Young People Board, Leader of the Opposition and chairperson of the Council's main scrutiny committee. 

Nick is passionate about the area and would be honoured to be Hexham's first MP from the Liberal tradition since Victor Finney (who served as MP from 1923-4).

My name is Alex Phimister, and I come from a family that has lived in Cramlington for generations.

As a former ambulance driver, I now work as a telecoms technician, climbing poles across the North East to provide for my family.

That’s why I’m standing as a Green Party candidate. Council tax takes a big chunk of people’s income, so local people deserve a say in how that money is spent and to see it invested back into our communities.

I’m really proud to be standing as the Labour candidate for Rowhill in the local elections on 7 May 2026.

It’s been a real privilege to support Councillor Lisa Greenway and the work she does for our community, and I’m very grateful to Cllr Alex Crawford for his guidance and support along the way. It’s also been a privilege to meet so many of you over the past year and hear about the issues that matter most to you.

If elected, I’ll continue working closely with Lisa and will always do my best to represent the people of Rowhill. I care deeply about the things that matter locally—keeping on top of potholes with Hampshire County Council, tackling fly-tipping, supporting community safety, and helping keep our neighbourhoods clean and welcoming.

I also understand how important it is that public money is looked after properly, and I’ll work to make sure Rushmoor’s finances continue to be managed carefully and responsibly.

Rowhill really is a special place, with fantastic people, community groups, green spaces, businesses and schools. I’m proud to be part of it, and I look forward to continuing to bring people together and support our community.

Your voice matters, and I’ll always listen.

I'm running in this council by-election because I want to stand up for my community and bring about real, positive change for all local residents.

I’ve felt the same frustrations with Conservative and then Labour run councils failing to deliver for us - whether that's the financial ruin the Tories left the council in, the continued lack of local housing and the lack of funding for leisure and green spaces.

I will advocate for people and planet on behalf of all the residents of Wellington to ensure that services are being run in the interests of us, the people, not investors and shareholders.

My priorities will be:

- Tackling the housing crisis by arguing for more public housing and responsible development

- Supporting local businesses to keep public money in our local community

- Safeguarding existing local services from council-wide budget cuts

- Ensuring residents, not private profit, shape decisions

- Seeking out and listening to your concerns - then acting on them

This by-election cannot change the control of the council. Whatever happens, Labour will continue in a minority administration.

This means they will need the support of others on the council to achieve anything, so whoever is elected will be able to hold them to account to deliver for residents.

You can decide if that new councillor is more of the same Tory and Labour politicians of the past, divisive Reform, or someone who wants to fight for residents.

Janine Lee is our Conservative Candidate for the upcoming Kinwarton by-election on July 9th. Many residents in Wilmcote already know Janine as Chair of the Parish Council, and also co-leader of the Bearley Wilmcote Action Group, who have been fighting the proposed housing in the local plan.

**Dear Resident,**

I’m so proud to have been selected as the Green Party candidate for the District Council by-election for Kinwarton on 9th July.

You may already know me if you went to school in Wilmcote or Haselor, or have hired Aston Cantlow Village Hall where I’m the caretaker and bookings manager. You may have seen me at the Cantlow Club with a drink, have visited my family business for a wig, or seen me walking our dogs around the local countryside. If so, you’ll know I’m someone who shows up and gets involved.

**Working hard in our community**

I was born and raised in this area and have been part of the community my whole life. I know our community is facing real challenges: flooding, the prospect of new builds on every horizon, and the fight to protect our green and pleasant environment. I’ve lived these issues alongside you. As a small business owner, former teacher and carer for my elderly parents, I know how to work hard, stay organised and push for solutions.

**Committed to fairness**

I’m standing to represent our villages because you deserve a councillor dedicated to fairness, community building, environmental protection and social justice. I’ll attend Parish Council meetings, hold regular drop‑ins, and take your concerns straight to the District Council.

Unlike other parties, the Green Party gives its councillors the freedom to stand by their communities – with no pressure from party officials, we’re at liberty to apply our values and work for what residents want.

**Vote for hope, locally and nationally**

Greens locally, regionally and nationally are working harder than ever to strengthen our communities and gain sustainable solutions. We have a track record of successful work with other parties, putting residents first.

We oppose the Stratford Liberal Democrat local plan. This will be destructive to many of our communities and remove Wilmcote’s green belt status. All development must be sustainable and infrastructure-led.

**Greens can win here!**

The Green Party won with 62% of the vote in Claverdon and Snitterfield, and now have 125 elected representatives across the West Midlands.

If you believe, like I do, that we can do better, that politicians should represent the people, then vote Green on 9th July.

**PS I can win, but every vote will count!**

Helen Elliott-Boult, is standing as Green Party candidate for Thrupp Ward (including Brimscombe, Bourne, Butterrow and part of Rodborough) for Stroud District Council.

Helen is a natural leader who shone during the pandemic, establishing our street WhatsApp networks, street reps, volunteers to collect food and prescriptions, keeping people connected online through quizzes and peer befrienders, and restoring a sense of resilience to Rodborough.

Helen sits on Parish Council as Vice-Chair, and as Chair, is instrumental in the actions of Environment and Amenities. She co-ordinates the Rodborough Repair Café, Sunday Social and has a key role in organising community events, including the Jubilee community celebrations, Warm Spaces, HAF events, the FORS Easter Egg hunts and Rodborough Fete.

In 2022, Helen called for a public conference to challenge the police and local government to do more to prevent violence against women and girls.

Helen works in person centred, Digital Inclusion, helping people to become more confident with the online world, and using digital tools to inspire people to imagine their community futures.

Helen is a community builder, a people person who can get things done, she is a quick-thinker, positive and empowering.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Terry is standing for election to Waverley Borough Council and Haslemere Town Council this May.

Originally from South Africa, where for many years he was an anti-apartheid campaigner, Terry is now a British citizen and has lived in the UK since 2003 (and in Haslemere since 2006).

Following a career in market research and later in education, Terry is now semi-retired, and works part-time delivering medicines for a local pharmacy, which has given him an acute awareness of the difficulties faced by some of the elderly and vulnerable people in our community. As a long-term rare cancer patient, he is a trustee for GISTCancerUK, and also a consumer representative with the NCRI Sarcoma Clinical Unit.

Terry has been a Haslemere town councillor since 2019, serving on the Climate and Biodiversity Emergency, Finance and Governance, and Planning committees - since 2022 as chair of the latter.

I am the Conservative Party candidate for the upcoming Haslemere Town, Waverley Borough, and Surrey County Council by-elections in our great town of Haslemere.

My family have called Haslemere 'home' for generations, and I want to give back to the town that has done so much for us by serving residents in Haslemere and the surrounding villages. As someone with business experience, I will bring economic competence and sound judgement to all of these councils, and will be a visible and active presence across Haslemere.

Alongside running monthly surgeries with residents and launching a new 'pothole patrol' to push for road repairs in our town to be prioritised, I will also campaign against the problematic plan put forward by the Liberal Democrats to develop on the Wey Hill car park site. I will also push for a fairer deal for so many commuters who are forced to pay eye-watering sums just to travel to work.

I am the Operations Director at a tech startup in London that I've helped build for the last 8 years, and a listening volunteer at Samaritans in Guildford. I am also the proud owner of a Countdown teapot, having won 8 games in a row on Channel 4's Countdown in 2024 (granting me the title of 'Octochamp'!).

Feel free to get in touch with me at toby4haslemere@gmail.com. And make sure to vote Conservative on July the 7th!

I have lived in Haslemere since 2009. Having grown up in a small town with experience in a family-owned business, I believe hard working, law-abiding taxpayers are the back  bone of the nation.

It has been of  great concern to observe the decline of the UK under previous and current governments, having abandoned British citizens by failing to deliver on their promises and to carry out the will of the people.

Some of the concerns that are affecting British citizens on a national and local level are:
* open borders and agenda to eliminate sovereign nations
* lawlessness and injustice in the judicial system
* loss of freedom of speech and religious expression (most notably Christian), loss 
  of parental authority, breakdown in the family unit.
* an absence of Christian principles which has underpinned the UK and other free 
 societies for centuries.
* decline in merit-based advancement, high taxation and government overreach.
* association with the European Court of Human Rights and WHO

These are just a few of the globalist/Marxist/secular humanist policies, along with social immorality which have contributed the the UK's decline--spiritually, economically, morally and militarily.

As a Christian, I believe in the return to long-held Christian values, which are pivotal in achieving liberty, prosperity and justice for all people.

I believe the Reformation of this country must begin at the local, grassroots level and this is why I am standing for West Surrey Council representing Haslemere.

These issues require boldness to promote truth, love for nation and to put the best interests of UK citizens and taxpayers first. REFORM UK is the only party with the bold, decisive leadership necessary to deliver this change in direction.

I will:
* Promote the preservation of Greenbelt land, local character, British culture and patriotism.
* Repair roads and update utilities in a timely manner--minimising disruption to local traffic.
* Challenge wasteful policies, releasing money into increasing local services.
* Support parental rights, while opposing destructive, woke ideology in the culture.

I will listen to your concerns and work on your behalf to produce an environment that is conducive to achieving a better quality of life for you, your children and grandchildren.

I will support and promote common sense policies that put the residents of Haslemere first, where decisions are made locally and stand against cronyism, government overreach and policies that subvert the public good.

**About me**

I'm Tom Shrive, your local Green Party candidate for the Haslemere triple by-election on 7th July. I live and work in Haslemere with my wife and young family, and I chose to raise my children here because it's such a special place.

I studied sustainable economics and run a technology business. I know how to manage budgets alongside social and environmental challenges, and I want to bring that experience to local government.

**My priorities**

When I speak with residents, the same priorities come up again and again:

- Protecting local services
- Ensuring council money is spent wisely
- Safeguarding the green spaces that make Haslemere what it is

If elected, I will fight to make sure any new development also invests in roads, community healthcare, schools and local facilities.

**What I will push for**

- A clear public tracker so residents can see where development money goes and what it delivers
- The proposed step-free bridge linking the Leisure Centre and Wey Hill to Tesco, making it easier to walk or cycle across town instead of driving
- Haslemere Town Council keeping control of local facilities, with proper budgets and full transparency over spending

I believe Haslemere's parks, woodlands and natural spaces must be protected for future generations, and that affordable housing for local families, young people and key workers should be delivered in the right places with the right infrastructure.

**Staying in touch**

I will hold ward surgeries both in person and online, so residents always have a way to raise local issues with me directly.

I was also secretary of the branch in Warwick, where I was also a committee member at the local Children's Centre and football club.

You are choosing a new local councillor in the l upcoming local council by-election on Thursday 5th March.

We are being let down by our existing local councillors, they are saying one thing and then doing the opposite.  Whether it's Labour councillors promising to not cut free public events, and 
then cancelling the fireworks. Or Reform Councillors saying they'll cut your Council Tax and then raising it. You need a better Councillor.

I'm a hardworking local man who like many have been struggling. I work full time for the NHS and I deeply care about people. I'm a former councillor who knows how to get things done and have a strong  track record of positive change for my area, including £100,000's of investment into the roads and hundreds of fixed potholes, new and improved play areas and local community groups receing funding in the tens of thousands. That is what I bring as your candidate and what I want to do for Spital.
The upcoming local council by-election is a chance to vote for change.
A  vote for me is a vote to invigorate our town centre and support small businesses. A vote for me is a vote to support local households who need help from the council, not council tax rises. A vote for me is a way to get a community champion elected to the council who is focused on tackling  issues like potholes, over development and traffic.

These are the issues I will focus on. What I offer is experience at scrutinising council decisions and uncovering hidden issues that will affect us all. I want 
to stop things like wasting public money and hiding decisions away from the public.

I will shine a light on the decisions being made that are hidden from you,  like Reforms County Councillors delaying flood prevention strategies and 
local road repairs and Labour Borough Councillors cutting more free town centre events.

This election will not change the political control of Tamworth Borough 
Council, but it is a chance to shine a light on what is happening at the 
council. 
Some candidates will promise you things they can’t deliver, or things that 
are nothing to do with the council. 
I promise you only what I can deliver. 
A vote for me in the upcoming by-election is a vote for someone who will 
act on local issues and be your community champion.

I will work hard for you both in the Council Chamber and in our 
neighbourhood. Including working with current councillors, police and community groups to form a community surgery.

Thank you for taking the time to hear from me and if you want to get in 
touch with me, please do drop me an email on
